## feat-db-transaction (2026-04-12)

Added `pool.transaction<T>(fn)` to the `DbPool` interface and implemented it in
`openDbPool`. The implementation is a single line: `writer.transaction(fn)(writer)`.
`better-sqlite3` handles BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK internally; if `fn` throws, it rolls
back and re-throws the original exception without wrapping. There is no catch block
in the wrapper — RC-1 is satisfied structurally, not by convention. The wrapper is
injected via `DbPool` (RC-3); no module-level transaction state exists. `fn` receives
`Database.Database` and returns `T`; the outer call returns `T` (AC-5, verified by
`tsc --noEmit`). 15 integration tests in `tests/storage/transaction.test.ts` cover
AC-1 (two-table write atomic after simulated mid-transaction crash, successful
commit, rollback + re-use), AC-2 (same Error instance re-thrown, no message
augmentation, non-Error throws pass through), AC-3 (reader cannot see uncommitted
WAL writes; sees them post-commit; sees nothing after rollback), and AC-5 (typed
return value, fn receives the writer connection). All 123 tests pass;
`tsc --noEmit` clean.

## feat-state-machine (2026-04-12)

Implemented the state machine module in full under `src/server/state-machine/`.
Three new TypeScript modules: `states.ts` (11-element `State` and 27-element
`Event` union literal types), `transitions.ts` (the `TRANSITIONS` const typed as
`{ [S in State]: Partial<Record<Event, TransitionResult>> }` — the mapped-type
key forces a compile-time error when a new State is added without a corresponding
entry; the `abandoned` row is typed as `satisfies Record<Event, TransitionResult>`
so adding a new Event also errors; every row is runtime-frozen via
`Object.freeze`; `transition()` is a pure lookup with no I/O), and
`classifier.ts` (`classify(stderr, parseState): FailureClass` — tags
rate-limit/overload patterns as `transient`, auth/permission patterns as
`permanent`, content-policy patterns as `policy`, and all other patterns as
`unknown`; parse state — parse_errors count + last event type — is a required
input per RC-3 and affects classification when the stream was wholly
unparseable). The `session_fail` TRANSITIONS entry has a conditional result with
three guarded outcomes: `classifier=transient → awaiting_retry`,
`classifier=permanent → awaiting_user`, `classifier=unknown → awaiting_user`,
so `unknown` can never reach a retry path. One known gap filed in handoff.json:
`(bootstrapping, user_cancel)` is absent from state-machine-transitions.md;
the test explicitly documents this as "not in the doc." 122 new tests across
`tests/state-machine/transitions.test.ts` (49) and `tests/state-machine/classifier.test.ts` (73);
all 245 tests pass; `tsc --noEmit` clean.

lines: +Implemented the Fastify HTTP + WebSocket server in four modules. `src/server/api/frames.ts` exports all protocol TypeScript types from protocol-websocket.md §8 and §2 payload shapes (`ServerFrame`, `ClientFrame`, all payload variants), plus `makeFrame()` and `makeErrorFrame()` factory helpers. `src/server/api/idempotency.ts` exports `IdempotencyStore` — an in-memory Map with explicit 5-minute TTL; `get()` evicts stale entries on access; `evictExpired()` for GC; no persistence (5-min window resets on restart, which is acceptable per spec). `src/server/api/ws.ts` exports `SessionSeqStore` (monotonic seq per sessionId starting at 1; non-session frames use seq:0), `BackfillBuffer` (in-memory ring buffer max 500 frames per session; returns `null` when sinceSeq predates retained history → caller emits backfill.truncated), and `createWsHandler(ctx)` — a factory that returns a per-connection handler implementing the full Yoke WS protocol: hello frame sent immediately on connect (AC-1); protocol version check → close 4001 on mismatch (AC-1); subscription cap of 4 concurrent workflowIds per client → error + close 4002 on the 5th subscribe (AC-3); subscribe sends workflow.snapshot then backfill frames or backfill.truncated with httpFetchUrl (AC-2); control commandId idempotency via IdempotencyStore (AC-4); ping → pong; ack is a no-op advisory. `src/server/api/server.ts` exports `createServer(db)` — registers `@fastify/websocket`, mounts the WS route at `/stream` on the root Fastify instance (not inside a sub-plugin scope to avoid decoration scoping issues), and registers all seven §7 HTTP companion endpoints: `GET /api/workflows` (keyset pagination), `GET /api/workflows/:id/timeline`, `GET /api/sessions/:id/log` (delegates to `readLogPage`), `GET /api/workflows/:id/usage`, `GET /api/workflows/:id/usage/timeseries`, `POST /api/workflows/:id/control` (idempotent), `POST /api/workflows/:id/attention/:attentionId/ack` (the one API-layer SQLite write); binds exclusively to 127.0.0.1 (D57 — `listenServer()` throws on any other host). All reads use `db.reader()`; only the attention ack endpoint calls `db.writer`. Two key implementation discoveries: (1) `@fastify/websocket` v8 passes `SocketStream` (a Duplex stream wrapper) to the handler, not the raw `WebSocket` — the raw socket is at `connection.socket`; (2) the WS hello frame can arrive in the same TCP read as the 101 upgrade response before any `message` listener is registered — solved by buffering messages in a queue from the moment the WebSocket is created (before `'open'` fires), making the `WsSession` test helper race-condition-safe. 59 new tests: 24 in `tests/api/fastify-ws.test.ts` (real Fastify server + real `ws.WebSocket` connections) and 35 in `tests/api/fastify-http.test.ts` (all §7 endpoints via `fastify.inject()`). All 763 tests pass; `tsc --noEmit` clean.

Implemented the Pre/Post Runner to satisfy AC-2. `src/server/prepost/action-grammar.ts` exports two pure functions: `resolveAction(actions, exitCode)` — resolves an exit code to its declared `ActionValue` by exact string key match then wildcard `"*"` fallback, returning `null` for undeclared codes; `isContinue(action)` — true iff the action is the `"continue"` sentinel. `src/server/prepost/runner.ts` exports `runCommands(opts)` — iterates the command array sequentially, spawning each `PrePostCommand` with `shell:false` in the worktree CWD; each command gets a per-command wall-clock timeout (default `DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_S = 900 s`) with SIGTERM→5 s→SIGKILL escalation; stdout is read line-by-line via `readline.createInterface` and each line is written as a `prepost.command.stdout` frame to the `SessionLogWriter`; stderr chunks are split on newlines and written as `prepost.command.stderr` frames; `prepost.command.start` and `prepost.command.exit` frames bracket each command with metadata (`name`, `when`, `cmd`, `ts`, `exit_code`, `elapsed_ms`); all writes go through a serialised `_writeQueue` promise chain (same pattern as `_logChain` in `JigSpawnHandle`) ensuring frame order is preserved and the queue is drained before returning — log completeness for the caller is guaranteed; resolution of exit code → `ActionValue` via `action-grammar.ts`; returns a `RunCommandsResult` discriminated union (`complete | action | timeout | spawn_failed | unhandled_exit`) for the Pipeline Engine to act on; no SQLite writes, no action execution in this module. The `SessionLogWriter` is caller-provided and already open — the runner does not open or close it, allowing it to be shared with the Process Manager for the agent session. 47 new tests: 22 pure unit tests in `tests/prepost/action-grammar.test.ts` (exact match, wildcard, no-match, `isContinue`) and 25 integration tests in `tests/prepost/runner.test.ts` (real node children, real tmpdir, real JSONL log: complete path, action returned, unhandled exit, spawn failure, JSONL frame content, env injection, timeout). All 704 tests pass; `tsc --noEmit` clean.

      "deferred_criteria": [
        "Pipeline engine live frame broadcast: createWsHandler and ServerState.seqStore/backfillBuffer are exposed for pipeline engine integration, but no code currently calls seqStore.next() or backfillBuffer.push() to emit live workflow.update/item.update/session.* frames. The pipeline engine (feat-scheduler or feat-server-main) must: obtain a reference to state.seqStore and state.backfillBuffer from createServer(); call seqStore.next(sessionId) for each frame, backfillBuffer.push(sessionId, frame), then broadcast to all subscribed WebSocket clients.",
        "WS client registry for broadcast: createWsHandler creates per-connection subscription state, but there is no registry that the pipeline engine can use to push frames to currently-connected clients. A broadcast mechanism (e.g. a Set<{socket, subscriptions}> shared via ServerState) must be added when the pipeline engine integration is implemented.",
        "http.ts deprecation: src/server/api/http.ts (the Node.js http.Server that served the session log HTTP route) is superseded by server.ts. It should be removed or marked deprecated when the orchestration layer migrates to createServer(). The two servers currently coexist and serve overlapping GET /api/sessions/:id/log paths.",
        "WS idempotency store is in-memory only: IdempotencyStore resets on server restart. The 5-minute TTL means that a client retrying a control commandId across a server restart within the TTL window will be re-executed rather than get the cached response. Acceptable per spec ('server restart resets idempotency window') but documented here for operator awareness.",
        "Attention ack is the one API-layer write: POST /api/workflows/:id/attention/:attentionId/ack is the only endpoint that calls db.writer. All other reads use db.reader(). If the attention ack write is moved to the pipeline engine in a future refactor, this module will become fully read-only."
      ],
      "known_risks": [
        "BackfillBuffer is an in-memory ring buffer (max 500 frames per session). On server restart, all backfill history is lost. Clients that reconnect after a restart will always receive backfill.truncated and must fall back to the HTTP /api/sessions/:id/log endpoint. This is documented behavior.",
        "The WS route registers directly on the root Fastify instance (not inside a sub-plugin scope) to avoid @fastify/websocket plugin scoping issues. If the server gains sub-application plugins that need the WS plugin decorated, they must also register on the root instance or the WS plugin must be re-registered in their scope.",
        "control commands are accepted (202/notice) and cached in IdempotencyStore but not executed: the pipeline engine integration is deferred. In the interim, POST /api/workflows/:id/control and WS control frames both return 'accepted' without actually changing any state. This is documented but creates a discoverability gap if a consumer assumes accepted means executed.",
        "The heartbeat interval (30 s) is hardcoded in ws.ts as HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS. The server sends no actual ping frames \u2014 the constant is declared in the hello frame's heartbeatIntervalMs field for the client to use as its keepalive timer. No server-side ping is implemented; if a client stops sending frames but stays connected, the server will not detect the stale connection until the OS closes the socket."
      ]
    }
  ],
  "prior_features": [

      "feature": "feat-session-log-store",
      "entries": [
        {
          "phase": "implement",
          "attempt": 0,
          "ts": "2026-04-13T08:30:00Z",

The Pipeline Engine must: (1) call makeSessionLogPath() using configDir + workflowId + sessionId to get the log path; (2) call db.writer.prepare(\"UPDATE sessions SET session_log_path = ? WHERE id = ?\").run(logPath, sessionId) before spawning (AC-4); (3) create SessionLogWriter(logPath), call open(), subscribe to SpawnHandle stdout_line events via writer.writeLine(), and call close() in the exit handler.",
        "Pre/Post Runner wiring (AC-2): The PrePostRunner module does not yet exist (src/server/prepost/runner.ts). When it is implemented, it must write command output frames to the same SessionLogWriter opened for the session (or open its own writer at the same logPath for pre-commands that run before the session spawns). The writer supports appending from multiple sequential writers at the same path.",
        "HTTP server integration: createHttpServer() implements the route using Node.js built-in http. When the full Fastify + WS server is scaffolded (feat-api-http or feat-server-main), this route must be registered as a Fastify route. The current http.Server factory is a standalone implementation; Fastify migration will require minor refactoring of the route handler.",
        "Retention policy enforcement (RC-2): The reader and writer respect retention.stream_json_logs config fields (max_age_days, max_total_bytes) by convention \u2014 no enforcement logic exists. A background cleanup task that scans ~/.yoke/<fingerprint>/logs/ and deletes files older than max_age_days or trims total bytes is deferred to a future maintenance feature.",
        "WS backfill path: the architecture doc mentions \"WebSocket backfill path\" backed by the session log. readLogPage() provides the correct interface for this but wiring it into the WS server is deferred to feat-api-ws."
      ],
      "known_risks": [
        "SessionLogWriter holds a FileHandle open for the duration of the session. If the process crashes without calling close(), the OS closes the file descriptor automatically (POSIX guarantees). However, data buffered in the Node.js stream that has not been flushed to the kernel may be lost. Each writeLine() call awaits fileHandle.appendFile() which calls write() then may not fsync. On crash, unflushed data (typically < 64 KB in the page cache) could be lost. For session log purposes this is acceptable \u2014 log incompleteness after a crash is documented and survivable.",
        "The reader reads a maximum of MAX_PAGE_SIZE (1000) + 1 lines from the file to detect hasMore. For very large log files (millions of lines), a sinceSeq near the end still causes the reader to stream past sinceSeq lines from the beginning to reach the target offset. For v1 session log sizes (thousands of lines), this is fast enough. A production optimization would use a byte-offset index, but this is deferred.",
        "createHttpServer() uses url.parse() (legacy Node.js API, not deprecated but discouraged). When the route is migrated to Fastify, this becomes a non-issue since Fastify parses URLs natively. For now, url.parse() is correct and well-tested.",
        "The HTTP server has no request timeout. A very slow file read (e.g., log file on a slow NFS mount) will hold the connection indefinitely. Acceptable for v1 (127.0.0.1 only, NFS unlikely); add a request timeout when the Fastify server is scaffolded."

        "**AC-4 / no session spawn write**: Nothing in production code calls `makeSessionLogPath()` and writes the result to `sessions.session_log_path`. Until a sessions row has this column populated, the HTTP endpoint always returns an empty page for any real session.",
        "**AC-2 / Pre/Post Runner absent**: `src/server/prepost/runner.ts` does not exist. If the Pre/Post Runner is a separate pending feature, this must be explicitly recorded as a handoff dependency. If it is in scope for this feature, it must be implemented."
      ],
      "non_blocking": [
        "**AC-6 test coverage (minor)**: The read-only tests at `session-log-http.test.ts:224` test `GET` on subtly different paths (`/log/write`, `/delete`) rather than issuing `POST`/`PUT`/`DELETE` on the actual `/api/sessions/:id/log` path. A method-level test (e.g., using `http.request({ method: 'POST' })`) would give stronger evidence.",
        "**`url.parse` deprecated (nitpick)**: `http.ts:107` uses `url.parse()`, which is deprecated since Node 11. `new URL(req.url, 'http://localhost')` is the current idiom.",
        "**Retention enforcement undocumented in source (nitpick)**: `writer.ts` has no comment pointing to `retention.stream_json_logs` as the governing config key. A one-line reference would satisfy RC-2's \"documented\" requirement at the code level, not just in `architecture.md`."
      ]
    },
    {

      "attempt": 1,
      "ts": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z",
      "intended_files": [

When implemented, it must call openSessionLog() (or open a SessionLogWriter at the same logPath returned by makeSessionLogPath()) and call writeLine() for each command output frame. SessionLogWriter supports appending from multiple sequential writers at the same path (O_APPEND semantics).",
        "AC-1 full orchestration: openSessionLog() provides the production hook for the orchestration layer. The orchestration loop (feat-scheduler / feat-server-main) must call openSessionLog(db, { configDir, workflowId, sessionId }) before calling ProcessManager.spawn(), then pass { logWriter: writer } in SpawnOpts. The wiring between these two modules is deferred.",
        "HTTP server integration: createHttpServer() still uses Node.js built-in http. When the full Fastify + WS server is scaffolded (feat-server-main), this route must be migrated to a Fastify route handler. The route logic in handleSessionLog() is self-contained and requires only minor refactoring.",
        "Retention policy enforcement (RC-2): retention.stream_json_logs is now referenced in the source comment but no enforcement logic exists. A background maintenance task scanning ~/.yoke/<fingerprint>/logs/ and deleting files past max_age_days or total-bytes limit is deferred to a future feature.",
        "WS backfill path: readLogPage() provides the correct paging interface for the WebSocket backfill path; wiring it into the WS server is deferred to feat-api-ws."
      ],
      "known_risks": [
        "The _logChain promise chain in JigSpawnHandle serializes writes but does not back-pressure readline. If writeLine() I/O is slower than stdout line production (e.g., on a slow disk), the chain grows unboundedly in memory. For v1 session rates (thousands of lines per session), this is not a practical concern.",

For a session that produces many lines, the drain adds a small wall-clock delay (file I/O for pending writes + close()). In crash-recovery scenarios where the child is killed, this delay is bounded by whatever is left in the chain at kill time.",
        "openSessionLog() calls db.writer directly (not inside a db.transaction()). If the caller needs to atomically write the sessions row AND set session_log_path, they must wrap both operations in their own transaction and call db.writer.prepare(...).run() directly rather than using openSessionLog().",

      "ts": "2026-04-13T13:07:31Z",

The criterion requires the runner to be implemented, not just planned. The handoff note is not a substitute for the code. Fix path: implement `prepost/runner.ts`; at runner startup, call `openSessionLog()` (or accept an already-open `SessionLogWriter` at the same `logPath`) and call `writer.writeLine(frame)` for each command output frame."
      ]

      "phase": "implement",
      "attempt": 2,
      "ts": "2026-04-13T09:30:00Z",
      "intended_files": [

        "src/server/prepost/runner.ts",
        "tests/prepost/action-grammar.test.ts",
        "tests/prepost/runner.test.ts"
      ],
      "fixes": [

      ],
      "deferred_criteria": [
        "AC-1 full orchestration: openSessionLog() provides the hook; the orchestration loop (feat-scheduler/feat-server-main) must call it before ProcessManager.spawn() and pass { logWriter: writer } in SpawnOpts. The wiring between openSessionLog() and runCommands() — specifically, sharing the same writer for pre-commands (before the session) and handing it off to ProcessManager.spawn() — is the orchestration loop's responsibility.",
        "prepost_runs table: runner.ts does not write to prepost_runs. The orchestration loop must insert a prepost_runs row and then fire the appropriate state-machine event via applyItemTransition. The runner returns RunCommandsResult for the caller to act on.",
        "HTTP server Fastify migration: createHttpServer() uses Node.js built-in http. When feat-server-main scaffolds Fastify, the handleSessionLog() route handler must be migrated; no logic changes required.",
        "Retention policy enforcement (RC-2): no background cleanup task exists. A future maintenance feature must scan ~/.yoke/<fingerprint>/logs/ and prune files per retention.stream_json_logs config.",
        "WS backfill path: readLogPage() provides the paging interface; wiring into the WS server is deferred to feat-api-ws."
      ],
      "known_risks": [
        "runCommands() accepts a caller-provided SessionLogWriter that is already open. For pre-commands that run before the session spawns, the orchestration loop must open the writer (via openSessionLog()), pass it to runCommands(), and keep it open for ProcessManager.spawn(). If the orchestration loop closes the writer between pre-commands and spawn, frames from the agent session will be lost. This contract must be documented in the orchestration layer.",
        "The SIGTERM\u2192SIGKILL escalation fires a 5 s background setTimeout after the outcome promise resolves. If the Node.js process exits within those 5 s (e.g., after a forced shutdown), the SIGKILL will not be delivered. For v1 (sequential, single-session), the child will be adopted and reaped by the OS. Acceptable risk.",
        "runOneCommand() uses child.on('close') (waits for all streams) rather than child.on('exit'). On Node.js 18+ this is correct. On older Node versions, 'close' may fire before the readline 'close' event in edge cases with very large stdout. The _writeQueue drain after settlement mitigates this — all queued writes complete before the result is returned.",
        "Empty run arrays (cmd.run = []) return spawn_failed immediately with a descriptive error. The config schema should validate that run has at least one element; if it does not, the runner is the last line of defence."
      ]
    }
  ],
  "prior_features": [
    {
      "feature": "feat-pipeline-engine",
      "entries": [
        {
          "phase": "implement",
          "attempt": 0,
          "ts": "2026-04-13T23:50:00Z",
          "intended_files": [
            "src/server/pipeline/retry-ladder.ts",
            "src/server/pipeline/engine.ts",
            "tests/pipeline/retry-ladder.test.ts",
            "tests/pipeline/engine.test.ts"
          ],
          "deferred_criteria": [
            "Orchestration event loop: applyItemTransition, checkStageComplete, and buildCrashRecovery are implemented and tested in isolation, but the scheduling loop that actually drives workflows (fires deps_satisfied when deps complete, fires backoff_elapsed after the timer elapses, fires rate_limit_window_elapsed after the reset_at timer, coordinates WorktreeManager/ProcessManager/PrePostRunner calls with the engine) does not yet exist. This is a higher-level concern deferred to a future integration feature (e.g., feat-scheduler or feat-server-main).",
            "Stage advancement writing to workflows.current_stage: checkStageCompleteInTxn returns true/false but does NOT write the next stage to workflows.current_stage. The orchestration loop must call db.writer.prepare('UPDATE workflows SET current_stage = ? WHERE id = ?') after detecting stageComplete=true from applyItemTransition. The engine intentionally does not do this because it does not know the next stage name (requires parsing the pipeline JSON from config).",
            "needs_approval stage pausing (RC-4): the engine detects stageComplete but has no code path to insert pending_attention{kind=stage_needs_approval} or pause the workflow at the workflow level. This requires the orchestration loop to check the needs_approval flag on the next stage config and call applyItemTransition at the workflow level with a stage_approval_granted event.",
            "Workflow-level state machine: workflows.status is never updated by the engine (only items.status is). The orchestration loop must update workflows.status when all items across all stages are terminal.",
            "backoff timer and rate_limit_window_elapsed timer: the engine returns retryMode and the caller must store it (e.g., in a local map or events table extra field) to pass as guardCtx.currentRetryMode when the backoff timer fires backoff_elapsed. A production scheduler would use setTimeout or a SQLite-backed schedule to fire these events. Neither is implemented.",
            "prepost_runs table: the engine writes events rows for every transition but does NOT write to prepost_runs. The Pre/Post Runner (not yet implemented) must write prepost_runs rows and then call applyItemTransition with the resulting action. The engine does not run pre/post commands itself.",
            "Artifact validators: applyItemTransition accepts validator_fail and diff_check_fail events but does not run ajv validators or items_from diff checks. These are caller responsibilities (the orchestration loop runs validators, then fires the appropriate event).",
            "WS frame emission: the sideEffects labels returned by applyItemTransition include labels like 'spawn agent session' and 'SIGTERM process group'. The caller (orchestration loop) is responsible for mapping these labels to concrete actions and for emitting workflow.update/item.update WebSocket frames after each transition."
          ],
          "known_risks": [
            "cascade_block BFS can produce N events rows for N transitive dependents in one transaction. In pathological graphs (e.g., a diamond DAG with 1000 dependents), this is a large write. better-sqlite3 synchronous writes are fast, but very deep/wide dependency trees may cause transaction latency visible as a heartbeat stall. V1 graphs are expected to be small (10\u201350 items).",
            "probeProcess(pid) uses process.kill(pid, 0) which on macOS may return EPERM for zombie processes (PID exists in the table but the process is effectively dead, waiting for parent to reap). The engine treats EPERM as 'alive', which is conservative \u2014 recovery_state won't be set for zombies. In practice, the harness uses detached children (child.unref()), so zombie accumulation is unlikely.",
            "The selectConditionalOutcome function indexes into the outcomes array by position (outcomes[0], outcomes[1], etc.). This coupling to the TRANSITIONS array structure means any reordering of outcomes in transitions.ts without corresponding reordering in engine.ts will silently pick the wrong outcome. A structural test asserting outcome ordering would catch this drift.",
            "buildCrashRecovery writes recovery_state to workflows but does NOT mark sessions as cancelled. A running session row with status='running' and a stale PID will remain as 'running' after crash recovery. The orchestration loop must update those sessions to status='cancelled' before restarting. If it does not, future crash recovery calls will re-detect the same stale PID repeatedly.",
            "The engine does not validate that the guardCtx.postCommandAction.goto destination phase actually exists in the stage's phase list. An invalid destination phase will be stored in items.current_phase and may cause confusion in the orchestration loop. Config-load-time validation of goto destinations (checking that the target is a real phase name in the same stage) would catch this early."
          ]
        },
        {
          "phase": "review",
          "attempt": 0,
          "ts": "2026-04-13T11:42:43Z",
          "verdict": "FAIL",
          "blocking_issues": [

lines: +        "Pipeline engine live frame broadcast: createWsHandler and ServerState.seqStore/backfillBuffer are exposed for pipeline engine integration, but no code currently calls seqStore.next() or backfillBuffer.push() to emit live workflow.update/item.update/session.* frames. The pipeline engine (feat-scheduler or feat-server-main) must: obtain a reference to state.seqStore and state.backfillBuffer from createServer(); call seqStore.next(sessionId) for each frame, backfillBuffer.push(sessionId, frame), then broadcast to all subscribed WebSocket clients.",

+        "WS client registry for broadcast: createWsHandler creates per-connection subscription state, but there is no registry that the pipeline engine can use to push frames to currently-connected clients. A broadcast mechanism (e.g. a Set<{socket, subscriptions}> shared via ServerState) must be added when the pipeline engine integration is implemented.",

+        "http.ts deprecation: src/server/api/http.ts (the Node.js http.Server that served the session log HTTP route) is superseded by server.ts. It should be removed or marked deprecated when the orchestration layer migrates to createServer(). The two servers currently coexist and serve overlapping GET /api/sessions/:id/log paths.",

+        "WS idempotency store is in-memory only: IdempotencyStore resets on server restart. The 5-minute TTL means that a client retrying a control commandId across a server restart within the TTL window will be re-executed rather than get the cached response. Acceptable per spec ('server restart resets idempotency window') but documented here for operator awareness.",

+        "Attention ack is the one API-layer write: POST /api/workflows/:id/attention/:attentionId/ack is the only endpoint that calls db.writer. All other reads use db.reader(). If the attention ack write is moved to the pipeline engine in a future refactor, this module will become fully read-only."

+        "BackfillBuffer is an in-memory ring buffer (max 500 frames per session). On server restart, all backfill history is lost. Clients that reconnect after a restart will always receive backfill.truncated and must fall back to the HTTP /api/sessions/:id/log endpoint. This is documented behavior.",

+        "The WS route registers directly on the root Fastify instance (not inside a sub-plugin scope) to avoid @fastify/websocket plugin scoping issues. If the server gains sub-application plugins that need the WS plugin decorated, they must also register on the root instance or the WS plugin must be re-registered in their scope.",

+        "control commands are accepted (202/notice) and cached in IdempotencyStore but not executed: the pipeline engine integration is deferred. In the interim, POST /api/workflows/:id/control and WS control frames both return 'accepted' without actually changing any state. This is documented but creates a discoverability gap if a consumer assumes accepted means executed.",

+        "The heartbeat interval (30 s) is hardcoded in ws.ts as HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS. The server sends no actual ping frames \u2014 the constant is declared in the hello frame's heartbeatIntervalMs field for the client to use as its keepalive timer. No server-side ping is implemented; if a client stops sending frames but stays connected, the server will not detect the stale connection until the OS closes the socket."

In projects with no remote (local-only), cleanup will always be refused unless checkPr is overridden to return true or the user uses 'yoke cancel' with --force.",

It would become a problem if two concurrent cleanup operations targeted the same worktree, which the Pipeline Engine must prevent by design.",

On macOS, if the child has called setsid() or setpgid() to move to a different process group, kill(-pid, SIGTERM) sends to the original pgid (which may be empty if all processes changed groups). This edge case can leave zombie processes. It is the same risk documented for ProcessManager.cancel() and is acceptable in v1.",

If a bootstrap command produces stdout exceeding 4 MB, execFileAsync will reject with an ENOBUFS-like error, which _runShellCommand catches and returns as { exitCode: 1, stderr: 'Spawn failed: ...' }. content.text:
Done. Here's the summary:

**Built** (`feat-fastify-ws`, commit `3e3e4aa`):
- `src/server/api/frames.ts` — protocol types + `makeFrame`/`makeErrorFrame` helpers
- `src/server/api/idempotency.ts` — 5-min TTL commandId store
- `src/server/api/ws.ts` — `SessionSeqStore`, `BackfillBuffer`, `createWsHandler` (hello, version check, subscribe/backfill/cap, control, ping, ack)
- `src/server/api/server.ts` — Fastify factory with WS route at `/stream` + all 7 §7 HTTP endpoints; 127.0.0.1 enforcement
- `tests/api/fastify-ws.test.ts` — 24 tests (real WS connections, race-condition-safe `WsSession` helper)
- `tests/api/fastify-http.test.ts` — 35 tests (all endpoints via `fastify.inject()`)

**763 tests pass; `tsc --noEmit` clean.**

content.text:
This is a stale background diagnostic script from the previous session — it was the debug tool used to identify the `SocketStream` vs `WebSocket` type issue (`connection.socket` keys shown at line 3-14: `_readableState`, `_writableState`, etc. — that's a Duplex stream, not a raw WebSocket). The 1006 close was expected since the debug script connected but didn't speak the protocol.

The issue it was investigating (`@fastify/websocket` passing `SocketStream` instead of `WebSocket`) was resolved in the implementation: `connection.socket` is passed to the handler instead of `connection` directly. All 763 tests pass. This task output can be ignored.
